title: Signals and Systems
summary: This page analyzes a circuit system that is neither controllable nor observable, deriving the state equation and output equation. The state equation q̇ = -0.5q is obtained by analyzing branch currents in the circuit. The page demonstrates how all circuit voltages and currents can be expressed in terms of the state variable q and input x.

figures.caption: Analysis of a system that is neither controllable nor observable.
figures.description: The figure shows two schematic circuit diagrams, labeled (a) and (b), used to analyze a system described as neither controllable nor observable. An input voltage source x(t) is applied on the left, the output y(t) is marked across the right-side terminals with + and - polarity, and nodes a, b, c, d, and e label the network connections; the circuit contains a 3 Ω resistor, four 2 Ω resistors, and in (b) a capacitor between a and b with charge q and current q̇. Students should notice the symmetry of the resistor network, the annotated branch currents such as x/5, x/10, and q̇/2, and how panel (b) introduces capacitor-state variables to relate internal dynamics to the same external input-output connection.
